critical.mandate: Story must be in review status before performance review
Auto-pass if no performance-relevant code changes detected
Report in percentiles (P50, P95, P99), not averages
Output must include machine-readable PASSED or FAILED verdict
After updating the Review Gate, if all 6 rows are PASSED and story is in 'review', you MUST transition story to 'done'

step.title: Load Story
step.action: Read story file, identify files changed
Extract the list of files changed from the story file

step.title: Status Gate
step.action: Verify story is in 'review' status
step.check: HALT: Story must be in review status before performance review

step.title: Auto-Pass Classification
step.action: Classify each changed file as performance-relevant or not:
Performance-relevant: files containing DB queries, API endpoints, data processing, rendering logic, loops/algorithms, middleware, caching, network calls
Not relevant: markdown (.md), config/yaml/json, test files, static assets, CSS-only, copy/translations, .gitignore, lock files
step.check: Verdict is PASSED with note "No performance-relevant code changes — auto-passed". Skip to step 8 (Generate Output) with this verdict.
step.action: If ANY performance-relevant files exist: proceed to full review (step 4)

step.title: N+1 and Database Analysis
step.action: Analyze for N+1 queries and inefficient database access patterns
Check for missing indexes on queried columns
Identify unbounded queries (missing LIMIT, pagination)

step.title: Memory and Bundle Analysis
step.action: Identify memory leaks, large payloads, unoptimized loops
Check frontend: bundle size impact, render blocking, unnecessary re-renders
Review image optimization and lazy loading

step.title: Caching and Complexity Review
step.action: Review caching strategy: what's cached, what should be, cache invalidation
Check for blocking operations and synchronous bottlenecks
Analyze algorithm complexity — flag O(n^2) or worse in hot paths

step.title: Verdict
step.action: If NO critical or high severity performance issues: verdict is PASSED
If ANY critical or high severity issue found: verdict is FAILED — list blocking findings

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate performance review report with: auto-pass classification result (if applicable), N+1 analysis, memory/bundle findings, caching review, algorithm complexity, and machine-readable verdict (PASSED/FAILED).

step.title: Update Review Gate
step.action: Read story file, locate Review Gate table

If Review Gate table is missing: initialize it with EXACTLY 6 rows — Code Review (PENDING), QA Tests (PENDING), Security Review (PENDING), Test Automation (PENDING), Test Review (PENDING), Performance Review (PENDING). Do NOT add any other rows.
If PASSED: update Performance Review row status to PASSED, link report file
If FAILED: update Performance Review row status to FAILED, link report file, set story status to 'in-progress' in story file and sprint-status.yaml
step.template-output: Update story file Review Gate table with performance review result and report link. If FAILED, also update story status to in-progress.

step.title: Transition Story If Gate Passed

critical.mandate: You MUST execute this step even if the Review Gate was already fully passed before this workflow run. The purpose of this step is to ensure the story status matches the gate state.

step.action: Re-read the story file now. Check two things: (1) the Review Gate table rows, (2) the current story status.
The Review Gate table must have EXACTLY 6 rows: Code Review, QA Tests, Security Review, Test Automation, Test Review, Performance Review. Remove any other rows.

If ALL 6 rows show PASSED AND the story status is still 'review': you MUST change the story status to 'done' in the story file AND in sprint-status.yaml. This is a REQUIRED write operation, not informational.
If ALL 6 rows show PASSED AND the story status is already 'done': log "Story already done. No update needed."
If any row is FAILED: log which reviews failed. Do not change story status.
If any row is PENDING: log which reviews are still pending. Do not change story status.
step.template-output: Read sprint-status.yaml. If all 6 Review Gate rows are PASSED and the story status is 'review', update the story status to 'done' and save the file.
